name: Teddy Kotick
abstract: Theodore John Kotick (June 4, 1928 – April 17, 1986) was an American jazz double bassist. Kotick never recorded as a leader, but he appeared as a sideman with many leading jazz musicians, including Charlie Parker, Buddy Rich, Artie Shaw, Horace Silver, Phil Woods, and Bill Evans.

concerts.label: Hommage à Jacques, "Jacques Brel is Alive and Well and Living in Paris"

works.t: Jacques Brel is Alive and Well and Living in Paris

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: He’d bring Teddy Kotick — it was Mitch Abelowitz’s gig... and John Early was the trumpet player... Gil used to come and sit in
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: I met Sam Jones, met Ike Isaacs who was playing with Carmen McRae at the time, Teddy Kotick with Horace Silver at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Bauer
about_quotes.evidence: I think Teddy Kotick was the bass player. So there was one in seven-eight time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Bauer interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: Nick Stabulus, and Teddy Kotick, and Gil Evans used to come sit in.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
